gloss:
On a three-level system there exist two states that stay indistinguishable for all time under both the tridiagonal measurement family and the position observable, yet are not related by any additive, real-scaling, modulus-preserving, population-preserving map. Below full tomographic measurement, dynamical indistinguishability is strictly coarser than symmetry equivalence.

**Cites** `WMSpec.LevelSystem.exists_dark_pair_wigner_gap` — the corpus theorem this result restates and discharges.

**Original contribution.** The raw lemma asserts existence of a dark pair; the thesis-layer statement reads it as the quantitative separation between two independently defined equivalences (dynamical vs symmetry) that only closes at tomographic completeness.

statement.pretty: ∀ (S₂ : WMSpec.LevelSystem 2), ∃ ψ χ, (∀ (t : ℝ), (TLT.NonIdentifiability.bisimilarity WMSpec.LevelSystem.tridiagFam (TLT.NonIdentifiability.pureKernel (WMSpec.LevelSystem.U S₂ t))) ψ χ) ∧ (∀ (t : ℝ), (TLT.NonIdentifiability.bisimilarity (fun x => WMSpec.LevelSystem.posN S₂) (TLT.NonIdentifiability.pureKernel (WMSpec.LevelSystem.U S₂ t))) ψ χ) ∧ ∀ (G : (Fin 3 → ℂ) → Fin 3 → ℂ), (∀ (φ₁ φ₂ : Fin 3 → ℂ), G (φ₁ + φ₂) = G φ₁ + G φ₂) → (∀ (r : ℝ) (φ : Fin 3 → ℂ), G (↑r • φ) = ↑r • G φ) → (∀ (φ : Fin 3 → ℂ) (n : Fin 3), Complex.normSq (G φ n) = Complex.normSq (φ n)) → (∀ (φ : Fin 3 → ℂ) (j : Fin 2), WMSpec.LevelSystem.wj j (G φ) = WMSpec.LevelSystem.wj j φ) → G ψ ≠ χ

title: Eidometry.Thesis.quantum_wigner_gap
